extension.description: Automatically render Opencode Sidebar Terminal in VS Code sidebar with full terminal support (fork of islee23520/opencode-sidebar-tui, with Windows compatibility improvements)

configuration.title: Opencode Sidebar TUI
configuration.ost.fontFamily.description: Terminal font family (Nerd Font recommended for Powerline/glyph support)
configuration.ost.scrollback.description: Maximum number of lines in scrollback buffer
configuration.ost.autoFocusOnSend.descri: Automatically focus the Open Sidebar after sending file references or text
configuration.ost.autoStartOnOpen.descri: Automatically start the AI tool when the sidebar is opened. When false, shows a tool selector each time.
configuration.ost.shellPath.description: Custom shell path to use for the terminal. Leave empty to use VS Code's default shell. Useful for specifying PowerShell, Git Bash, or other shells on Windows.
configuration.ost.shellArgs.description: Custom arguments to pass to the shell. Leave empty for automatic detection. Example: ["/c"] for cmd.exe or ["-Command"] for PowerShell.
configuration.ost.sendKeybindingsToShell: Send Ctrl/Cmd + letter shortcuts (including Ctrl+P) directly to the sidebar terminal instead of letting VS Code intercept them. This extension's main purpose is running opencode (a TUI with its own keybindings), so the default is ON. You can turn it off if you prefer normal IDE shortcuts even when the sidebar terminal is focused. Control keys like Ctrl+C are always sent to the terminal.
configuration.ost.showTmuxWindowControls: Show direct tmux session and window controls (+S, previous window, +W, next window) in the terminal toolbar. Turn this off to keep only the tmux command menu.
configuration.ost.autoShareContext.descr: Automatically share editor context (open files, selections) with OpenCode
configuration.ost.httpTimeout.descriptio: HTTP API request timeout in milliseconds for communication with OpenCode
configuration.ost.enableHttpApi.descript: Enable HTTP API for bidirectional communication between VS Code and OpenCode
configuration.ost.logLevel.description: Log output level for Opencode Sidebar TUI
configuration.ost.terminalBackend.descri: Select the terminal backend. Unavailable backends safely fall back to native.
configuration.ost.terminalBackend.enumDe: Native terminal — basic shell only, no multiplexer features (panes, tabs, sessions)
Tmux — full session, pane, and window management via tmux
Zellij — full session, pane, and tab management via zellij
configuration.ost.collapseSecondaryBarOn: Close the secondary sidebar before moving the terminal into an editor group so the new editor tab gets the full workspace width.
configuration.ost.codeActionSeverities.d: Diagnostic severities that trigger code actions
configuration.ost.aiTools.description: Configure AI coding tools. Use Add Item to add new tools, Edit to modify, Delete to remove.
configuration.ost.aiTools.items.label.de: Display name shown in the tool selector
configuration.ost.aiTools.items.path.des: Path to the executable. Empty = resolve from PATH
Alternative names that resolve to this tool
configuration.ost.aiTools.items.operator: Operator ID for tool-specific launch and routing behavior
configuration.ost.defaultAiTool.descript: Name of the default AI tool to launch when creating a new terminal session
Show the AI tool selector popup when attaching to or creating a tmux/zellij session
configuration.ost.pane.defaultSplitDirec: Default direction when splitting a terminal pane
configuration.ost.pane.focusOnClick.desc: Focus a pane when clicking on it
configuration.ost.pane.showPaneActions.d: Show pane action buttons (split, close) in the terminal
configuration.ost.pane.renderer.descript: Terminal renderer: webgl (fast), canvas (fallback), or auto (webgl up to 4 panes, then canvas)
